key_insight: The paper turns PINN residuals into deterministic, pointwise error intervals by using a mesh-based compatible finite-element reconstruction and adjoint-based error estimation.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical way to certify local solution accuracy in physics-informed models, which is especially valuable for solid mechanics and other safety-critical PDE applications.
method: Reconstruct an admissible PINN prediction as a piecewise-linear compatible field on a mesh.
Apply the discrete numerical system to this field to obtain a certifying residual and pointwise error estimate.
Use exact adjoint Green formulas when possible, with norm-based, localized, randomized, and nonlinear extensions when exact correction is impractical.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests on manufactured examples, a large-scale 3D elasticity benchmark, and projected neural load families to assess compatibility and calibration.
watch_item: The strongest guarantees rely on mesh compatibility and may require additional transfer estimates or approximations when moving from discrete targets to the continuous PDE solution.
paper_title: Pointwise Error Estimates for Numerical Physics-Informed Neural Networks
